mainHtml:
Resources/Process Guide

Manufacturing Process Guide

Focusing on process knowledge, comparison tables, applicable scenarios and related capability recommendations, we help customers determine a more suitable manufacturing route before quoting.

OverviewProcess comparisonData preparation

How to choose between CNC, 3D printing and sheet metal?

Early evaluation typically begins with part construction, materials, accuracy, quantity, and surface requirements. Through structured judgment items, customers can narrow down the candidate process scope faster.

It is recommended to first confirm the use of parts, stress environment, target quantity, key dimensions and appearance requirements.

Process comparison

CNC machiningHigh precision and wide material coverage, suitable for functional prototypes and batch parts.
3D PrintingSuitable for complex structures, fast verification and low-volume iterations.
Sheet metal formingSuitable for enclosures, stands, chassis and lightweight structures.

Data preparation

It is recommended to prepare 3D models, 2D drawings, quantities, materials, Surface Finishing and inspection data requirements before submitting a quote.

Improve Early-Stage Project Decisions

Process selection usually needs to be judged together with materials, Surface Finishing, and testing data. It is recommended to complete the information in the following order.

Look at the materialConfirm strength, weight, corrosion resistance, temperature and cost boundaries.
fixed surfaceClarify appearance, wear resistance, anti-corrosion, color and masking area requirements.
Ready for testingState critical dimensions, reporting formats, and acceptance criteria ahead of time.

Not sure which process to choose?

Send us the parts usage, quantity and key requirements, and the engineering team will first make a manufacturing route judgment.

Submit requirements
